_schema: principle_family → past Tyler video that demonstrates it + which natural materials can invoke it
_note: Only principles that pass the Material Constructibility test from Doc 5 v2 are included. The 'rejected' section lists principles from Tyler's catalog that CAN'T be invoked from natural materials (magnet boots, Vantablack, D3O, Doc Oc) — usable for tutorial-style videos, not riddle-hook videos.

constructible.construction_method: Mix cornstarch + water at ~2:1 by volume to make oobleck. Soak fabric in it. Apply to body. Soft when stretched slowly, locks rigid on impact.

past_videos.title: How much can this make me lift?
Making a real life STRENGTH suit

constructible.construction_method: Rope routed over sticks acting as fixed/movable pulleys. Each loop halves the pull force needed. Long pole + rock pivot = lever with mechanical advantage = load arm / effort arm ratio.

principles: composite material layering for impact resistance
interlaminar shear failure in cross-grain laminates

past_videos.title: How I made BULLETPROOF Batman Armour

constructible.construction_method: Stack flexible material at alternating fiber orientations (0°/45°/90°/-45°), bond every other layer with tape or wet starch. Cross-grain distributes impact across multiple directions.

principles: thermal insulation via cellulose + water + pyrolysis char composite

past_videos.title: Making the coolest Halloween costume ever 😳

constructible.construction_method: Layer: outer mud (char layer + thermal mass) → middle wet organic (water absorbs heat via vaporization latent heat 2260 J/g) → inner substrate (low thermal conductivity). Multi-layer shield exploits radiation reflection + specific heat + phase change + char insulation.

principles: elastic potential energy storage in compressible media
constructible.construction_method: Compressible medium + rigid platform on top + falling weight. Stored elastic PE = compression energy released as kinetic energy on rebound. ~90-95% energy return for good elastomers.

principles: displacement volume = weight of fluid displaced

past_videos.title: How much can this make me lift?
past_videos._note: tangential — but the strength-lifting tribe of videos covers force-multiplication generally

constructible.construction_method: Lash empty sealed containers together. Total buoyancy = volume × water density. Distribute load above. Self-rights if metacenter is above center of mass.

principles: parabolic load distribution under uniform weight

past_videos.title: Da Vinci Bridge (Reciprocal)
past_videos._note: Reciprocal is adjacent to catenary — both are no-fastener structural mechanisms.

constructible.construction_method: Suspend flexible tension member between two anchors. Under self-weight it forms a catenary (no point load) or parabola (uniform load). Strongest possible shape for those loads. Walk-the-rope or load-bearing suspension bridge.

principles: Jurin's law height of capillary rise
constructible.construction_method: Porous fibrous material wicks water against gravity via meniscus formation in narrow channels. Useful for moving water without pumps, filtering, or condensation collection.
constructible._note: Tyler may have no direct video — use only when paired with strong adjacent past principle

constructible.construction_method: Spin a wood spindle rapidly against a wood baseboard. Friction heats the contact point > ignition temp of dust. Tinder catches.
Rub two materials from opposite ends of the triboelectric series. Electrons transfer; one becomes negative, the other positive. Generates 10s of kV in dry conditions. Can spark to tinder or shock.
Match driving frequency to object's natural frequency. Amplitude builds with each cycle. Reaches destructive amplitude (glass shattering, bridge collapse — Tacoma Narrows).
Spin a weight on a rope. At release, the weight flies off tangentially at v = ωr. Higher angular velocity = more force. Sling weapon (David and Goliath).
Tube with one end submerged at higher elevation, the other lower. Once primed, atmospheric pressure pushes fluid up the short leg; gravity pulls it down the long leg. No pump needed.

past_videos.title: Climbing with MAGNET Shoes
past_videos._note: Adjacent — kick-out mechanism uses CoM positioning

constructible.construction_method: Position mass so the center of gravity falls inside the base of support. For an overhung beam, counterweight on the opposite side moves CoM back over support.

rejected_for_riddle_use.why_rejected: Requires neodymium magnets / electromagnets that aren't lying around. Material Plausibility = 0.
Carbon nanotube coating isn't constructible from natural materials. Material Sufficiency = 0.
Specialty polymer. Can use the general shear-thickening principle via cornstarch (oobleck) instead — see non_newtonian_shear_thickening family above.
Tendon-driven robot can't be hand-built in the moment from natural materials.
